Event: Sayid immediately begins getting a signal fire going after the crash
Explanation: He increases his and the group's chances of rescue with no downside shown.

Event: Sayid ropes Charlie into helping with the signal fire
Explanation: He recruits help to build the fire faster, benefiting both himself and the group.

Event: Sayid insists on seeing Essam buried rather than cremated
Explanation: He upholds religious burial rites, giving others respect and closure while modestly affirming his own values.

Event: Sayid prays on a few occasions on the Island
Explanation: His prayers give him some comfort/grounding without affecting others much.

Event: After his rescue and Nadia’s death, Sayid abandons his religious observance and drinks alcohol
Explanation: His coping strategy harms himself (self-destructive drift from values) with no clear benefit to others.

Event: Sayid goes rogue in Season 5, assaulting Jin
Explanation: He asserts his own agenda while physically and socially harming an ally and the group’s cohesion.

Event: Sayid shoots a 12-year-old Ben in cold blood in 1970s
Explanation: He gains no lasting benefit and instead creates the very enemy he fears and endangers his friends’ cover.

Event: Sayid briefly allies with Jack again during the plan to set off the bomb
Explanation: Rejoining the plan gives him purpose and aids the group’s broader escape strategy.

Event: Sayid defects to the Man in Black in Season 6
Explanation: He seeks personal relief/power by joining an antagonist at the expense of his former allies’ safety.

Event: Sayid makes deals with Ben
Explanation: He gains direction and some revenge opportunities while helping Ben’s often harmful schemes.

Event: Sayid makes deals with the Man in Black
Explanation: He pursues personal desires (like promised resurrections) while empowering a destructive entity against others.

Event: Sayid tortures prisoners as an interrogator for the Republican Guard
Explanation: He maintains career/utility to his regime while seriously harming others.

Event: Sayid beats up Ben after Ben claims not to know where his “wife” was buried
Explanation: He vents rage and gains brief emotional release while inflicting disproportionate harm.

Event: Sayid becomes Ben’s “Dragon” (enforcer) after leaving the Island
Explanation: He gains employment/purpose yet helps Ben terrorize or kill others.

Event: Sayid becomes the Man in Black’s Dragon with Claire in Season 6
Explanation: He gets protection/role but furthers a force that endangers the survivors.

Event: Sayid helps Jack as a technological “Lancer” in Season 4
Explanation: He uses his expertise to support Jack’s leadership, improving survival and rescue chances for all.

Event: Sayid uses military training and combat skill to protect himself and others
Explanation: His prowess keeps him alive and often directly defends his companions.

Event: Sayid constructs radio transmitters and gadgets from salvaged parts
Explanation: His technical improvisation boosts communication and survival for both him and the group.

Event: Sayid properly questions the “suicide” story of Locke’s death
Explanation: His skepticism aligns with the truth and could guide others away from trusting the wrong people.

Event: Sayid goes on a Roaring Rampage of Revenge against Widmore’s inner circle on Ben’s orders
Explanation: He satisfies personal vengeance and obeys Ben but murders many, yielding no broader good.

Event: Sayid shoots young Ben, triggering Ben’s handover to the Others
Explanation: The attempt to avert future evil instead causes Ben’s transformation and destabilizes the timeline.

Event: Sayid serves as a hitman for Ben after Nadia’s death
Explanation: He gains purpose and an outlet for grief while causing lethal harm to others.

Event: Sayid more or less gives up on hope of redemption in late Season 5
Explanation: Abandoning redemption worsens his self-destructiveness without aiding anyone else.

Event: Post-infection, Sayid becomes apathetic when Claire tries to cut Kate’s throat
Explanation: His refusal to intervene preserves his status with the Man in Black while endangering an ally.

Event: Sayid flashes a terrifying psychotic smirk post-infection
Explanation: He embraces a menacing persona for psychological dominance, unsettling others with no constructive outcome.

Event: The Man in Black recruits Sayid as Dragon by promising to bring Nadia or Shannon back
Explanation: He manipulates Sayid’s grief to gain a powerful servant at great risk to others.

Event: Sayid has a talk with Desmond that reawakens his better nature
Explanation: Desmond helps Sayid recover his conscience, benefitting them both and those around them.

Event: Dogen declares Sayid “infected” and aligns him with the Man in Black’s side
Explanation: His classification and treatment push Sayid toward the enemy, undermining group safety.

Event: Roger Linus shoots Sayid
Explanation: He protects his interests and retaliates, harming Sayid and contributing to further chaos.

Event: Others drown Sayid in the healing water of the temple
Explanation: Their risky act revives him, apparently meant to heal, though it has mixed later effects.

Event: Sayid switches to the Man in Black’s side after being declared infected
Explanation: He chooses alignment that secures him a role but directly threatens his former allies.

Event: Sayid helps Desmond in the well (implied cooperation after their talk)
Explanation: Regaining empathy, he aids Desmond and thereby works against the Man in Black.

Event: Sayid takes the bomb on the submarine and runs deeper into the sub with it
Explanation: He knowingly sacrifices his life to drastically reduce casualties, offering others a last chance to survive.

Event: Sayid heroically sacrifices himself, dying in the submarine explosion
Explanation: His death saves key companions at the cost of his own life.

Event: Sayid awakens after being dead for a few hours in the temple pool
Explanation: His return from death restores a powerful ally, at least initially, benefiting the group.

Event: Sayid dies in the submarine as a redeemed version of himself
Explanation: He gains moral redemption but loses his life while preserving his friends.

Event: Sayid immediately resumes leadership/initiative roles after the crash (signal fire, planning)
Explanation: His early leadership and planning improve everyone’s survival prospects, including his own.
